decisions.decision: Treat the in-scope write surface as the full five-route POST set resolved by `resolve_write_route_category(...)`: create market, post comment, resolve market, probability edit, and event trade. Unsupported POST paths stay outside the rate-limit-header contract.
alternatives_rejected: Limit the task to the two order routes only.
Expand header expectations to unsupported POST paths that never pass the protected-write classifier.

decisions.decision: Keep the shared `handle_api()` plus `rate_limit_headers(...)` contract as the single source of truth: emit `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`, and `X-RateLimit-Policy` on successful protected-write responses and on `429 rate_limit_exceeded`, while continuing to omit quota headers for disabled-limiter and missing-agent cases and not broadening 400/409 application errors in this task.
alternatives_rejected: Add route-local header plumbing inside each write handler.
Expand quota headers to every post-gate 400/409 application error.

decisions.decision: Preserve the current quota-accounting order where `_enforce_write_controls()` spends quota before route-specific idempotency replay or terminal rejection logic. This task only freezes the visible header contract; it does not change whether replays or rejected protected writes consume quota.
alternatives_rejected: Special-case idempotent replays so they do not consume quota.
Charge quota only for newly accepted writes.

decisions.decision: Implement this task primarily as HTTP contract coverage in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`, because the shared adapter already emits success-path headers across the protected POST surface. Keep the existing ProbabilityEdit tests as the shared proof for decrement math, `Retry-After`, `429`, and per-agent isolation, and add route-breadth success assertions for the other write endpoints instead of duplicating the full 429 matrix everywhere.
alternatives_rejected: Restructure backend write handlers despite the current shared adapter already covering the behavior.
Duplicate the full rate-limit exhaustion matrix for every protected write route.

steps.task: Audit `backend/server.py` to keep `BayesHandler.handle_api()` and `rate_limit_headers(...)` as the only header-emission seam for protected POSTs, and patch backend code only if the new route-breadth tests expose a gap in that shared path.

Extend the threaded HTTP test scaffolding in `BayesMarketApiAuthRateLimitTests` with small request helpers or inline bodies for create-market, comment-post, market-resolve, and event-trade submissions so each protected write route can be exercised with a valid `X-Bayes-Agent-Id` and positive `RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN`.

Add or update success-path header assertions for all five protected POST routes, including changing the stale resolve success expectation to header presence when the limiter is enabled, and adding first-class coverage for comment-post plus explicit breadth checks for create-market and event-trade.

Keep the existing absence-path assertions for missing or invalid agent IDs, disabled limiter mode, and unsupported POST routes, and rely on the current ProbabilityEdit tests as the shared regression seam for remaining-quota decrement, `429` responses, `Retry-After`, and per-agent isolation.
Run the focused backend HTTP auth/rate-limit test slice, review the resulting diff to confirm the task stayed backend-and-tests only, and commit the completed worktree with the requested message.
